diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient developed jaundice, ascites, and body weight gain after chemotherapy. A liver biopsy showed sinusoidal dilatation and congestion, but no central vein stenosis or obstruction initially. Autopsy findings later confirmed hepatic SOS with fibrosis of the sinusoids, bile congestion, and stenosis of the central vein. The clinical manifestations and pathological findings post-autopsy supported the final diagnosis of hepatic SOS.
